etymology.description: semantic 心(心) (xīn, "heart") + phonetic 青(青) (qīng) -> emotions; feelings
etymology.interpretation: The character 情(qíng) combines the semantic component 心(心) (xīn, "heart"), representing emotions and feelings, with the phonetic component 青(青) (qīng) to suggest the pronunciation. The heart is considered the seat of emotions, thus naturally leading to the character's meaning of "emotions; feelings."

examples.chinese: 他的情感非常丰富(他的情感非常豐富)。
她对这部电影没有任何情感(她對這部電影沒有任何情感)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì zhè bù diànyǐng méiyǒu rènhé qínggǎn.
examples.english: She has no feelings towards this movie.

examples.chinese: 在写信时(在寫信時)，表达自己的情感很重要(表達自己的情感很重要)。
examples.pinyin: Zài xiě xìn shí, biǎodá zìjǐ de qínggǎn hěn zhòngyào.
examples.english: When writing a letter, it's important to express one's emotions.
